zh.Combe Martin, Devon, England: 英國戴文可姆 馬丁
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness於1422年出生在英國戴文可姆 馬丁時，她的父親William Martin22歲，母親Lady Esther20歲。她和Sir Thomas Channon至少有1個兒子。她去世於1450年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.Combe Martin, Devon, England: 英国戴文可姆 馬丁
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness于1422年出生在英国戴文可姆 馬丁时，她的父亲William Martin22岁，母亲Lady Esther20岁。她和Sir Thomas Channon至少有1个儿子。她去世于1450年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness was born in 1422, in Combe Martin, Devon, England, her father, William Martin, was 22 and her mother, Lady Esther, was 20. She had at least 1 son with Sir Thomas Channon. She died after 1450, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness est née en 1422, à Combe Martin, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Martin, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lady Esther, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Thomas Channon. Elle est décédée après 1450, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness 1422 in Combe Martin, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Martin 22 und ihre Mutter Lady Esther 20. Sie hatte mit Sir Thomas Channon mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1450 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness è nata il nel 1422 nel Combe Martin, Devon, England, sua padre, William Martin, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Lady Esther, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Thomas Channon. Morì dopo il 1450, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Combe Martin, Devon, England: イギリスデボンコンベ マルティン
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Martin Baronessが1422年にイギリスデボンコンベ マルティンで生まれた時，父William Martinは22歳，母Lady Estherは20歳であった。彼女はSir Thomas Channonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1450年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness(이)가 1422년 영국 데폰 코브 마틴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Martin(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Esther(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas Channon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1450년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness nasceu em 1422, em Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Martin, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Lady Esther, tinha 20 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Thomas Channon. Ela faleceu após 1450, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness родилась в 1422 г. в местности комб мартинь, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени William Martin было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Esther, 20. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Thomas Channon. Она умерла после 1450 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Martin Baroness nació en 1422, en Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Martin, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Lady Esther, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Thomas Channon. Murió después de 1450, en su pueblo.
